description_long: This is a medium-sized, roughly oval stone with a smooth, weathered surface and subtle color variations ranging from beige to light tan. The stone exhibits a natural, irregular shape with some visible cracks and minor surface imperfections, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. One side appears slightly flatter, while the rest of the body is more rounded and bulbous, giving it a somewhat asymmetrical profile. There are faint, natural mineral streaks and patches of lighter and darker tones, with a small area showing a remnant of a label or marking. The stone's surface is mostly matte with a few slightly glossier patches where the texture is smoother. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A smooth, oval-shaped beige stone with subtle cracks and natural color variations, showing signs of weathering and minor surface imperfections.
description_view_0: The stone appears rounded and bulbous, with a smooth, slightly matte surface and some visible cracks near the top.
description_view_1: This side reveals a more pronounced edge and a visible label or marking, with rougher texture along the seam.
description_view_2: The stone looks flatter and more oval, with the label more clearly visible and subtle color mottling across the surface.
description_view_3: The back view shows a more elongated, teardrop shape with a continuous, smooth surface and less visible cracking.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle natural mottling and faint mineral streaks, with irregular, organic distribution and no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Smooth and slightly polished with minor cracks and surface imperfections, indicating moderate weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with landscaped elements, potentially partially embedded in soil or surrounded by grass or mulch.
